name: High-Temperature Adaptation
description: A Reverse-Entropy Core is built from Alpha-grade architecture and a dangerous thermodynamic anomaly stack. The hotter its operating environment, the faster its cognition, modeling, and strategic adaptation become. These gains are useful, but regulators note that high-temperature colonies can resemble covert training grounds for an intelligence already too close to human initiative.
author: Domain AI Core Management Regulations, Article 29

name: Reverse-Entropy Backflow
description: "If the core needs this much heat to think, connect it to the ship. Let the flux grid pay the bill."
author: Unattributed engineering log
